task.title: Open Control Room browser from Pi
task.summary: Improve /wiki-ui so Pi attempts to open the local Control Room URL in the system browser and always reports a copyable URL fallback.

goal.outcome: Users can access the Control Room from Pi without manually copying a URL when browser launching is available.
acceptance: /wiki-ui attempts to open the Control Room URL in the system browser after starting or reusing the local server.
If automatic browser launch is unavailable or fails, /wiki-ui keeps the server running and reports a clear copyable URL.
The reported URL remains a plain http://127.0.0.1 link for terminals that auto-link URLs.
Browser-launch command construction is covered by tests without opening a real browser.
non_goals: No broad OS automation beyond opening the local URL.
No change to Control Room server semantics.

summary: TASK-062 /wiki-ui browser launch and URL fallback

evidence: Extended control-room.test.mjs to verify platform-specific browser opener commands and fallback URL message formatting.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added buildBrowserOpenCommand, tryOpenUrlInBrowser, and formatControlRoomLaunchMessage to the Pi /wiki-ui command.
Updated docs/specs to say /wiki-ui opens the browser when available and always prints a plain URL fallback.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Extended control-room.test.mjs to verify platform-specific browser opener commands and fallback URL message formatting.
code_change_evidence: Added buildBrowserOpenCommand, tryOpenUrlInBrowser, and formatControlRoomLaunchMessage to the Pi /wiki-ui command.
Updated docs/specs to say /wiki-ui opens the browser when available and always prints a plain URL fallback.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: /wiki-ui attempts to open the Control Room URL in the system browser after starting or reusing the local server.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: registerUiCommand now calls tryOpenUrlInBrowser for both existing and newly started server URLs.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: If automatic browser launch is unavailable or fails, /wiki-ui keeps the server running and reports a clear copyable URL.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: formatControlRoomLaunchMessage reports browser failure and includes 'Open: http://127.0.0.1:<port>/'; server is registered before open attempt and remains active.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: The reported URL remains a plain http://127.0.0.1 link for terminals that auto-link URLs.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: formatControlRoomLaunchMessage returns plain URL text; tests assert fallback includes http://127.0.0.1:3000/.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Browser-launch command construction is covered by tests without opening a real browser.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: control-room.test.mjs asserts macOS open, Linux xdg-open, Windows cmd /c start, and unsupported platform null.
npx tsc --noEmit and npm test passed.

closure_brief.user_intent: Let Pi open the Control Room browser automatically, or at minimum make the local URL easy to click/copy.
implemented_changes: /wiki-ui now attempts to open the local Control Room URL using the platform browser opener.
/wiki-ui reuses existing running servers and attempts to open their URL too.
Fallback status message always includes a plain http://127.0.0.1 URL.
Docs/specs describe browser opening plus URL fallback.
acceptance_evidence: Tests cover browser opener command construction on darwin/linux/win32/unsupported platforms.
Tests cover fallback URL message formatting.
non_goals_preserved: No broad OS automation beyond opening the local URL.
No change to Control Room server semantics.
remaining_risks: Headless/SSH environments may spawn an opener that still cannot show a browser; fallback URL remains available.

risks: Automatic browser launching can still fail after process spawn in headless or remote environments; the plain URL fallback remains the reliable access path.
